title: Felicia hirta
intro: Felicia hirta, also known as Felicia or Yellow Felicia, is a flowering plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant is traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Felicia hirta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Felicia hirta is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or inflorescence decoction, and is also used in traditional remedies such as ointments and creams for skin conditions.
taxonomy: Felicia hirta is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant is commonly known as Felicia or bush daisy. Felicia hirta is native to southern Africa, where it is found in a variety of habitats. The plant is a shrubby perennial that produces small white or purple flowers in clusters. It is often used as an ornamental plant due to its attractive blooms and tolerance of dry conditions.
